title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepts table-qualified column mappings
summary: The Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepted dotted identifiers in column mapping values.
observed: A column mapping could use a schema-qualified or table-qualified shape instead of a single column name.
expected: Column mappings must be single Oracle MySQL column identifiers.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for dotted column mappings.
impact: Over-qualified column names make generated write SQL harder to reason about and are not part of the supported mapping contract.
delivery_semantics: The fix rejects invalid SQL generation before runtime delivery work starts.
security: Keeping table identifiers and column identifiers distinct reduces injection and review risk.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL column mappings reject dotted identifiers.
Table names continue to support table and database.table forms.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL SQL tests.
documentation: Document that column mappings are single-column identifiers.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the SQL validation fix is published.
